comment.body: It appears there’s a clear negative speed profile above 6725, with traders positioned for an upside bias. Dealers are long the butterfly (long near ATM and short the wings), though higher realized volatility is expected on the upside wing. If we rebound from current levels and can test and hold above 6750, price could stabilize around 6780, with a potential push toward 6800—though that move is unlikely to be sustained, as liquidity near 6785 may act as a magnet. On the downside, a test of 6660 is possible; if that level holds, we would likely reverse back toward the center of the fly. Overall it does look entire zone between 6650-6800 is short gamma signaling illiquidity with more liquidity underneath.

comment.body:
he says this bcs of the local long charm profile.
but 143 futs (if his calculation is accurate), isn't too much.
and ask yourself: whats the matter when there is long charm low volume and spx still goes up? or ehwn there is short charm and spx still goes down?
it doesn't have that much impact.
positioning matters only to monitor the sentiment: why the participants placed their bets where they placed? strucutrally... 
you need to see the structure, the context. then the positioning, then the changes of the positions.

MM hedging flow itself is not as important. They shift quick if position shifts. 
But understanding the structure and the bet on liquidity, helps you to see what the participants expect and want to see... thats why we look for discrepancies.

(but regarding your email that you sent me, i don't know why you ask my opinion...)

comment.body:
Is this the case ? 
https://x.com/VolSignals/status/2001370782354804896?s=20
Why can't the SPX find a bid yet today?Because without someone stepping in to buy hard delta (big blocks of futures), market makers have ~ 143 futures to sell every five minutesjust because the clock ticked.
